aliases: cold Horlicks with condensed milk and powder

english: cold Horlicks with condensed milk and powder

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for Horlicks Dinosaur; mapped to the closest parent class 'malted milk beverage' (FOODON_03301099). The Dinosaur preparation (cold, with condensed milk and heaped undissolved powder topping) is a Singaporean hawker-specific variant not represented in FoodOn.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Malted beverages) is the closest defensible anchor. The Dinosaur preparation variant is not individually coded in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for malted milk beverage preparations of this type.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 g (one cup or glass), as typically served at Singaporean hawker centres and kopitiams.

nutrient_source.title: Horlicks Dinosaur — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Horlicks Dinosaur (cr_id D04020008). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from the HPB Foodo database for comparable condensed-milk-based malted beverages, as the exact saturated fat breakdown was not separately reported in the primary entry; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database entry for malted milk beverages with added sugar
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Horlicks Dinosaur specifically. GI of 55 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweetened malted milk beverages (Horlicks-type drinks, GI approximately 52–57). Per the University of Sydney GI Database classification, GI ≤55 is categorised as 'low'. The condensed milk addition and heaped undissolved powder topping increase the effective glycaemic load. GL per serving calculated as: GI (55) × carbohydrate per serving (35.15 g from 250 g × 14.06 g/100 g) / 100 = 19.3. This is an analogous estimate; direct measurement is recommended.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (8.81 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage format independently elevates blood pressure risk; sodium at 86 mg/100 g is moderate but cumulative
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Horlicks Dinosaur due to its sugar-sweetened beverage status (8.81 g sugar/100 g), which independently raises blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidance, and should request omission of condensed milk and the extra powder topping to reduce sugar load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 8.81 g sugar/100 g, low-to-medium GI (~55), and high glycaemic load per serving (~19.3); condensed milk and heaped undissolved powder topping further elevate postprandial glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Horlicks Dinosaur as it is a sugar-sweetened beverage with a GI of approximately 55 and a high glycaemic load of ~19.3 per 250 g serving; the condensed milk and extra Horlicks powder topping significantly elevate postprandial blood glucose and should be omitted or substituted with unsweetened alternatives.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Added sugar from condensed milk and malted powder contributes to dyslipidaemia and cardiovascular risk; saturated fat from condensed milk is an additional concern
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Horlicks Dinosaur due to its high added sugar content (8.81 g/100 g) from condensed milk and malted powder, which contributes to dyslipidaemia and elevated triglycerides, and its saturated fat content from condensed milk, which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol levels.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High added sugar (8.81 g/100 g) and saturated fat from condensed milk promote systemic inflammation; no anti-inflammatory components present
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Horlicks Dinosaur as its high added sugar content and saturated fat from condensed milk promote pro-inflammatory pathways, and the absence of omega-3 fatty acids, polyphenols, or antioxidants provides no anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Horlicks Dinosaur is a popular cold malted milk beverage served at Singaporean hawker centres and kopitiams. It is prepared by dissolving Horlicks malted milk powder in cold water or cold milk, adding sweetened condensed milk for richness and sweetness, and then finishing with a heaped tablespoon of undissolved Horlicks powder piled on top — the signature 'dinosaur' presentation that gives the drink its name. The undissolved powder topping is meant to be stirred in by the drinker or consumed as a thick, sweet paste. The drink is sweet, creamy, and malty in flavour, with a thick texture from the condensed milk and the powder topping. Per 100 g, it provides 73 kcal, 2.05 g protein, 0.89 g fat, 14.06 g carbohydrate, 8.81 g sugar, 0.37 g dietary fibre, and 86 mg sodium. The high sugar content (8.81 g/100 g) classifies it as a sugar-sweetened beverage, making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low by University of Sydney classification), with a glycaemic load of approximately 19.3 per 250 g serving. The condensed milk adds saturated fat and concentrated sugar, further elevating the cardiometabolic risk profile. This drink is a Singaporean cultural icon and is widely recognised across Chinese, Malay, and Tamil-speaking communities in Singapore.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D04020008) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from comparable HPB entries for condensed-milk-based malted beverages. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened malted milk beverages. Multilingual names are well-established for this Singaporean hawker drink.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.55 g/100 g) is estimated from HPB Foodo comparable entries for condensed-milk-based malted beverages, as the exact saturated fat breakdown was not separately reported in the primary HPB entry for D04020008. Direct measurement is recommended. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient breakdown, and moisture data for this specific Horlicks Dinosaur preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened malted milk beverages (Horlicks-type); per the University of Sydney GI Database classification scale, GI ≤55 is categorised as 'low'. Direct GI measurement for Horlicks Dinosaur is not available in any approved source; direct measurement is recommended. 4. The 'dinosaur' preparation is a Singaporean hawker-specific variant where a heaped tablespoon of undissolved Horlicks powder is placed on top of the cold drink, increasing the effective sugar and carbohydrate intake beyond the per-100 g values reported here. 5. Dietary tags 'low_sodium' and 'low_calorie' from the input have been reviewed: sodium at 86 mg/100 g is moderate-low, and energy at 73 kcal/100 g is relatively low per 100 g, but a 250 g serving delivers 182.5 kcal and 215 mg sodium, which is not clinically low. These tags are retained in cultural context only and are not used in clinical guidance.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed from HPB source data.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match_type fields from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' to satisfy G5.

Iteration 3: Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' to match glycemic_index of 55. Per the University of Sydney GI Database classification, GI ≤55 is categorised as 'low'. Updated gi_source notes and llm_training description to reflect correct 'low' GI category. Updated type2_diabetes key_factor to reflect 'low-to-medium GI' phrasing for clinical accuracy.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
